questions.question: Which ancient Indian text is considered the earliest known work of Indian literature?
questions.explanation: The Rigveda is the oldest of the Vedas and is considered one of the earliest texts in Indian literature, dating back to around 1500 BCE.

questions.question: Who was the prominent leader of the Non-Cooperation Movement launched in 1920?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi led the Non-Cooperation Movement as part of the Indian independence struggle in 1920.

questions.question: The Battle of Plassey in 1757 was fought between the British East India Company and which Indian ruler?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Plassey was fought between the British East India Company and Nawab Siraj-ud-Daula of Bengal; Mir Jafar was allied with the British.

questions.question: Which movement marked the beginning of the modern Indian freedom struggle in 1857?
questions.explanation: The Revolt of 1857, also known as the Sepoy Mutiny, is considered the first major challenge to British rule, marking the beginning of the modern freedom struggle.

questions.question: Which river is known as the 'Sorrow of Bihar' due to frequent flooding?
questions.explanation: The Kosi River is notorious for flooding Bihar, causing widespread destruction, earning it the nickname 'Sorrow of Bihar.'

questions.question: Which is the largest coastal state of India by length?
questions.explanation: Gujarat has the longest coastline among Indian states, approximately 1600 km long.

questions.question: The Thar Desert primarily covers which Indian state?
questions.explanation: The Thar Desert is mainly located in Rajasthan.

questions.question: Which mountain range forms the northern boundary of India?
questions.explanation: The Himalayas form the northern boundary of India, acting as a natural barrier.

questions.question: Who is the current President of India as of 2026?
questions.explanation: Droupadi Murmu is the current President of India since 25 July 2022.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with the Fundamental Rights?
questions.explanation: Articles 12 to 35 of the Indian Constitution deal with Fundamental Rights.

questions.question: Who has the authority to declare a constitutional emergency in India?
questions.explanation: The President of India has the authority to declare a constitutional emergency under Article 352.

questions.question: The Lok Sabha is composed of members elected directly by the people for a term of how many years?
questions.explanation: Members of the Lok Sabha are directly elected for a period of 5 years.

questions.question: The term 'Gross Domestic Product' (GDP) refers to the
options: Total value of goods and services produced within a country
questions.explanation: GDP measures the total value of goods and services produced within a country's borders.

questions.question: The 'Gross Budgetary Support' in the Indian Budget primarily refers to funds allocated for
questions.explanation: Gross Budgetary Support includes funds allocated for developmental projects and subsidies.

questions.question: Which financial institution is responsible for regulating the banking sector in India?
questions.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is the regulator of banking in India.

questions.question: Which planet is known as the 'Red Planet'?
questions.explanation: Mars is called the 'Red Planet' due to its reddish appearance caused by iron oxide on its surface.

questions.question: The process of photosynthesis in plants primarily involves which pigment?
questions.explanation: Chlorophyll is the main pigment involved in photosynthesis, capturing light energy.

questions.question: Who is known as the 'Father of the Indian Space Programme'?
questions.explanation: Vikram Sarabhai is regarded as the father of the Indian space program.

questions.question: Which device is used to measure the intensity of electric current?
questions.explanation: An ammeter is used to measure electric current.

questions.question: Which of the following is a non-renewable resource?
questions.explanation: Coal is a non-renewable resource because it takes millions of years to form.

questions.question: The Convention on Biological Diversity (CBD) was held in which year?
questions.explanation: The CBD was held in 1992 in Rio de Janeiro, focusing on conservation of biodiversity.

questions.question: Which national park is known for having the largest population of Bengal tigers in India?
questions.explanation: Sundarbans is famous for its large Bengal tiger population.

questions.question: What is the primary cause of acid rain?
options: Emission of sulfur dioxide and nitrogen oxides
questions.explanation: Acid rain is primarily caused by sulfur dioxide and nitrogen oxides released into the atmosphere.

questions.question: Who is the current Chief Minister of Odisha as of 2026?
questions.explanation: Mohan Charan Majhi has been serving as the Chief Minister of Odisha since 12 June 2024.

questions.question: Who is the current External Affairs Minister of India?
questions.explanation: S. Jaishankar is the current External Affairs Minister of India.

questions.question: Who is the current Vice President of India as of 2026?
questions.explanation: C. P. Radhakrishnan is the Vice President since 12 September 2025.

questions.question: Which international organization launched the 'Green Climate Fund' to assist developing countries?
questions.explanation: The UNFCCC established the Green Climate Fund to support climate change mitigation in developing countries.

questions.question: Which sports event is scheduled to be hosted by India in 2023?
questions.explanation: India is scheduled to host the Asian Games in 2023.

questions.question: Who founded the Indian National Congress?
questions.explanation: The Indian National Congress was founded by Allan Octavian Hume in 1885.

questions.question: Which resource is primarily found in the Raniganj coalfield?
questions.explanation: The Raniganj coalfield is famous for its extensive coal deposits.
